author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XLI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the people, on the Feast of the Martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The solemn day of the Martyrs hath dawned; therefore to the glory of the Passion of Christ, the Captain of Martyrs, who spared not Himself, ordering His soldiers to the fight; but first fought, first conquered, that their fighting He might encourage by His example, and aid with His majesty, and crown with His promise: let us hear somewhat from this Psalm pertaining to His Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I commend unto you oftentimes, nor grieve I to repeat, what for you is useful to retain, that our Lord Jesus Christ speaketh often of Himself, that is, in His own Person, which is our Head; often in the person of His Body, which are we and His Church; but so that the words sound as from the mouth of one, that we may understand the Head and the Body to consist together in the unity of integrity, and not be separated the one from the other; as in that marriage whereof it is said, “They two shall be one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then we acknowledge two in one flesh, let us acknowledge two in one voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First, that which responding to the reader we have sung, though it be from the middle of the Psalm, from that I will take the beginning of this Sermon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine enemies speak evil of Me, When He shall die, then shall His Name perish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the Person of our Lord Jesus Christ: but see if herein are not understood the members also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was spoken also when our Lord Himself walked in the flesh here on earth.…When they saw the people go after Him, they said, “When He shall die, then shall His Name perish;” that is, when we have slain Him, then shall His Name be no more in the earth, nor shall He seduce any, being dead; but by that very slaying of Him shall men understand, that He was but a man whom they followed, that there was in Him no hope of salvation, and shall desert His Name, and it shall no more be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He died, and His Name perished not, but His Name was sown as seed: He died, but He was a grain, which dying, the corn immediately sprang up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When glorified then was our Lord Jesus Christ, began they much more, and much more numerously to trust in Him; then began His members to hear what the Head had heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then our Lord Jesus Christ being in heaven set down, and Himself in us labouring on earth, still spake His enemies, “When He shall die, then shall His Name perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hence stirred up the devil persecutions in the Church to destroy the Name of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unless haply ye think, brethren, that those Pagans, when they raged against Christians, said not this among themselves, “to blot out the Name of Christ from the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Christ might die again, not in the Head, but in His Body, were slain also the Martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the multiplying of the Church availed the Holy Blood poured forth, to help Its seminating came also the death of the Martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of His Saints.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More and more were the Christians multiplied, nor was it fulfilled which spake the enemies, “When He shall die, then shall His Name perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now also is it spoken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Down sit the Pagans, and compute them the years, they hear their fanatics saying, A time shall come when Christians shall be none, and those idols must be worshipped as before they were worshipped: still say they, “When He shall die, then shall His Name perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Twice conquered, now the third time be wise!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ died, His Name has not perished: the Martyrs died, multiplied more is the Church, groweth through all nations the Name of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who foretold of His own Death, and of His Resurrection, He who foretold of His Martyrs’ death, and of their crown, He Himself foretold of His Church things yet to come, if truth He spake twice, has He the third time lied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Vain then is what ye believe against Him; better is it that ye believe in Him, that ye may “understand upon the needy and poor One;” that “though He was rich, yet for your sakes He became poor, that ye through His poverty might be rich.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is he that understandeth upon the needy and poor One: in the evil day shall the Lord deliver him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the evil day will come: will thou, nill thou, come it will: the Day of Judgment will come upon thee, an evil day if thou “understand not the needy and poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what now thou wilt not believe, shall be made manifest in the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But neither shalt thou escape, when it shall be made manifest, because thou believest not, when it is kept secret.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Invited art thou, what thou seest not to believe, lest when thou see, thou be put to the blush.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Understand then upon the needy and poor One,” that is, Christ: understand in Him the hidden riches, whom poor thou seest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Him are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thereby in the evil day shall He deliver thee, in that He is God: but in that He is man, and that which in Him is human hath raised to life, and changed for the better, He hath lifted (thee) to heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He who is God, who would have one person in man and with man, could neither decrease nor increase, neither die nor rise again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He died out of man’s infirmity, but God dieth not.…But as we rightly say, Such a man died, though his soul dieth not; so we rightly say, Christ died, though His Divinity dieth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore died?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because needy and poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not His death offend thee, and avert thee from beholding His Divinity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is he that understandeth upon the needy and poor One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider also the poor, the needy, the hungry and thirsty, the naked, the sick, the prisoners; understand also upon such poor, for if upon such thou understand, thou understandest upon Him who said, “I was an hungred, I was thirsty, I was a stranger, naked, sick, in prison;” so in the evil day shall the Lord deliver thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And deliver him not into the hand of his enemy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The enemy is the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none think of a man his enemy, when he hears these words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Haply one thought of his neighbour, of him who had a suit with him in court, of him who would take from him his own possession, of him who would force him to sell to him his house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not this; but that enemy think of, of whom said the Lord, “an enemy hath done this.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He it is who suggests that for things earthly he be worshipped, for overthrow the Christian Name this enemy cannot.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he hath seen himself conquered by the fame and praises of Christ, he hath seen, whereas he slew Christ’s Martyrs, that they are crowned, he triumphed over.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath begun to be unable to persuade men that Christ is nought; and because by reviling Christ, he now with difficulty deceives, by lauding Christ, he endeavours to deceive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before this what said he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom worship ye?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Jew, dead, crucified, a man of no moment, who could not even from himself drive away death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When after His Name he saw running the whole human race, saw that in the Name of the Crucified temples are thrown down, idols are broken, sacrifices abolished; and that all these things predicted in the Prophets are considered by men, by men with wonder astonished, and closing now their hearts against the reviling of Christ; he clothes himself with praise of Christ, and begins to deter from the faith in another manner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is the law of Christ, powerful is that law, divine, ineffable! but who fulfilleth it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the name of our Saviour, “tread upon the lion and the dragon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By reviling openly roared the lion; by lauding craftily lurks the dragon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them come to the faith, who doubted; and not say, Who fulfilleth it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If on their own strength they presume, they will not fulfil it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Presuming on the grace of God let them believe, presuming (on it) let them come; to be aided come, not to be judged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So live all the faithful in the Name of Christ, each one in his degree fulfilling the commands of Christ, whether married, or celibates and virgins, they live as much as God granteth them to live; neither presume they in their own strength, but know that in Him they ought to glory.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord help him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Haply in heaven, haply in the life eternal, that so it remain to worship the devil for earthly needs, for the necessities of this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast “promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came unto thee on earth, by Whom were made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider then what He saith, “The Lord help him, on his bed of pain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bed of pain is the infirmity of the flesh; lest thou shouldest say, I cannot hold, and carry, and tie up my flesh; thou art aided that thou mayest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord help thee on thy bed of pain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy bed did carry thee, thou carriedst not thy bed, but wast a paralytic inwardly; He cometh who saith to thee, “Take up thy bed, and go thy way into thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord help him on his bed of pain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then to the Lord Himself He turneth, as though it were asked, Why then, since the Lord helpeth us, suffer we such great ills in this life, such great scandals, such great labours, such disquiet from the flesh and the world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He turneth to God, and as though explaining to us the counsel of His healing, He saith, “Thou hast turned all his bed in his infirmity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the bed is understood anything earthly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every soul that is infirm in this life seeketh for itself somewhat whereon to rest, because intensity of labour, and of the soul extended toward God, it can hardly endure perpetually, somewhat it seeketh on earth whereon to rest, and in a manner with a kind of pausing to recline, as are those things which innocent ones love.…The innocent man resteth in his house, his family, his wife, his children; in his poverty, his little farm, his orchard planted with his own hand, in some building fabricated with his own study; in these rest the innocent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet God willing us not to have love but of life eternal, even with these, though innocent delights, mixeth bitterness, that even in these we may suffer tribulation, and so He turneth all our bed in our infirmity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast turned all his bed in his infirmity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him not then complain, when in these things which he hath innocently, he suffereth some tribulations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is taught to love the better, by the bitterness of the worse; lest going a traveller to his country, he choose the inn instead of his own home.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He “scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because to men sinning was it said, “In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore because all these chastisements, in which all our bed is turned in our infirmity, man ought to acknowledge that he suffers for sin; let him turn himself, and say what follows: “I said, Lord, be merciful unto me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, by tribulations do Thou exercise me; to be scourged Thou judgest every son whom Thou wilt receive, who sparedst not even the Only-Begotten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He indeed without sin was scourged; but I say, “I have sinned against Thee.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine enemies speak evil of Me, When He shall die, then shall His Name perish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this we have already spoken, and from this began.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And entered in to see” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Christ suffered, that suffereth also the Church; what the Head suffered, that suffer also the Members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the disciple is not above his Master, nor the servant above his Lord.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If to Christ’s Members thou belongest, come within, cling to the Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Endure the tares if thou art wheat, endure the chaff if thou art grain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Endure the bad fish within the net if thou art a good fish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore before the time of winnowing dost thou fly away?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore before the time of harvest, dost thou root up the corn also with thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore before thou art come to the shore, hast thou broken the nets?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They go abroad, and tell it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All mine enemies whisper against Me unto the same thing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against Me all unto the same thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much better with me unto the same thing, than against me “unto the same thing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Against me unto the same thing”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With one counsel, with one conspiring.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ then speaketh unto thee, Ye consent against Me, consent ye to Me: why against Me? wherefore not with Me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That same thing if ye had always had, ye had not divided you into schisms.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, saith the Apostle, “I beseech you, brethren, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no division among you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All mine enemies whisper against Me unto the same thing:” against Me do they “devise evil to Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To themselves rather, for “they have gathered iniquity to themselves;” but therefore to Me, because by their intention they are to be weighed: for not because to do nothing was in their power, to do nothing was in their will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the devil lusted to extinguish Christ, and Judas would slay Christ; yet Christ slain and rising again, we are made alive, but to the devil and to Judas is rendered the reward of their evil will, not of our salvation.…The intention wherewith they spake, not what they spake, did He consider, who related that they spake evil of Him, “Against Me they devised evil to Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what evil to Christ, to the Martyrs what evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All hath God turned to good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “An ungodly word do they set forth against Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of ungodly word?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen to the Head Itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come, let us kill Him, and the inheritance shall be ours.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall the inheritance be yours?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because ye killed Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo! ye even killed Him; yet shall not the inheritance be yours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall not He that sleepeth add this also, that He rise again”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When ye exulted that ye had slain Him, He slept; for He saith in another Psalm, “I slept.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They raged and would slay Me; “I slept.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If I had not willed, I had not even slept.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I slept,” because “I have power to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I laid Me down and slept, and rose up again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rage then the Jews; be “the earth given into the hands of the wicked,” be the flesh left to the hands of persecutors, let them on wood suspend it, with nails transfix it, with a spear pierce it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall He that sleepeth, not add this, that He rise up again?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore slept He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “Adam is the figure of Him that was to come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Adam slept, when out of his side was made Eve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Adam in the figure of Christ, Eve in the figure of the Church; whence she was called “the mother of all living.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When was Eve created?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While Adam slept.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When out of Christ’s side flowed the Sacraments of the Church?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While He slept upon the Cross.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The man of My peace, in whom I trusted, which did eat of My bread, hath enlarged his heel against Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): hath raised up his foot against Me: would trample upon Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is this man of His peace?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in him did Christ trust, that He said, “in whom I trusted”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He not know him from the beginning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He not before he was born know that he would be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had He not said to all His disciples, “I have chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then trusted He in him, but that He is in His Members, and that because many faithful trusted in Judas, the Lord transferred this to Himself?…“The man of My peace, in whom I trusted, which did eat of My bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How showed He him in His Passion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the words of His prophecy: by the sop He marked Him out, that it might appear said of him, “Which did eat of My bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, when he came to betray Him, He granted him a kiss, that it might appear said of him, “The man of My peace.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, O Lord, be merciful unto Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the person of a servant, this is the person of the needy and poor: for, “Blessed is he that understandeth upon the needy and poor One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See, as it was spoken, “Be merciful unto Me, and raise Me up, and I will requite them,” so is it done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Jews slew Christ, lest they should lose their place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ slain, they lost their place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rooted out of the kingdom were they, dispersed were they.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He, raised up, requited them tribulation, He requited them unto admonition, not yet unto condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the city wherein the people raged, as a ramping and a roaring lion, crying out, “Crucify Him, Crucify Him,” the Jews rooted out therefrom, hath now Christians, by not one Jew is inhabited.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is planted the Church of Christ, whence were rooted out the thorns of the synagogue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For truly this fire blazed “as the fire of thorns.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Lord was as a green tree.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This said Himself, when certain women mourned Christ as dying.…“For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in a dry?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When can a green tree be consumed by the fire of thorns?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they blazed as fire among thorns.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fire consumeth thorns, but whatsoever green tree it is applied to, is not easily kindled.…Yet lest ye think that God the Father of Christ could raise up Christ, that is, the Flesh of His Son, and that Christ Himself, though He be the Word equal with the Father, could not raise up His own Flesh; hear out of the Gospel, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But,” said the Evangelist (lest even after this we should doubt), “He spake of the temple of His Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Raise Me up, and I will requite them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By this I know that Thou favourest Me, that Mine enemies shall not triumph over Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the Jews did triumph, when they saw Christ crucified; they thought that they had fulfilled their will to do Him hurt: the fruits of their cruelty they saw in effect, Christ hanging on the Cross: they shook their heads, saying, “If Thou be the Son of God, come down from the Cross.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came not down, who could; His Potency He showed not, but patience taught.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if, on their saying these things, He had come down from the Cross, He would have seemed as it were to yield to them insulting, and not being able to endure reproach, would have been believed conquered: more firm remained He upon the Cross, than they insulting; fixed was He, they wavering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therefore shook they their heads, because to the true Head they adhered not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He taught us plainly patience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mightier is that which He did, who would not do what the Jews challenged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For much mightier is it to rise from the sepulchre, than to come down from the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That Mine enemies shall not triumph over Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They triumphed then at that time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ rose again, Christ was glorified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now see they in His Name the human race converted: now let them insult, now shake the head: rather now let them fix the head, or if they shake the head, in wonder and admiration let them shake.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But as for Me, Thou upholdest Me, because of Mine innocence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly innocence; integrity without sin, requiting without debt, scourging without desert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou upholdest Me because of Mine innocence, and hast made Me strong in Thy sight for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast made Me strong for ever, Thou madest Me weak for a time: Thou hast made Me strong in Thy sight, Thou madest Me weak in sight of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise to Him, glory to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is the God of Israel, our God, the God of Jacob, the God of the younger son, the God of the younger people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none say, Of the Jews said He this, I am not Israel; rather the Jews are not Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the elder son, he is the elder people reprobated; the younger, the people beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The elder shall serve the younger:” now is it fulfilled: now, brethren, the Jews serve us, they are as our satchellers, we studying, they carry our books.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear wherein the Jews serve us, and not without reason.…With them are the Law and the Prophets, in which Law, and in which Prophets, Christ is preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When we have to do with Pagans, and show this coming to pass in the Church of Christ, which before was predicted of the Name of Christ, of the Head and Body of Christ, lest they think that we have forged these predictions, and from things which have happened, as though they were future, had made them up, we bring forth the books of the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews forsooth are our enemies, from an enemy’s books convince we the adversary.…If any enemy clamour and say, “Ye for yourselves have forged prophecies;” be the books of the Jews brought forth, because the elder shall serve the younger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therein let them read those predictions, which now we see fulfilled; and let us all say, “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting, and all the people shall say, So be it, So be it.”
